memory.summary: I've got a scholarship deadline looming, but I'm tempted to skip the hard work for this fun solo event right now. My close friends might get mad if I miss out.
I finally see the math: starting early actually reduces Thursday stress, so I'll do it now and save that fun time for later when my friends are around.
The assignment link is dead, which ruins my plan to do it now and save the fun for later with friends. I need to ask them about this immediately.
The broken link ruins my solo work plan, but I can't risk missing the deadline or disappointing my friends. I'll message hallmate_A right now to fix this before things get worse.
Thanks for the help, but now I realize half my time is wasted and the link is still broken. I need to tell them this immediately so we can fix it before Thursday.
Wait, the link is still broken after my message? I need to tell them immediately so we can fix it before Thursday.
My hallmate said thanks, but the link is still broken. I need to tell them this immediately so we can fix it before Thursday.
They said it's fixed, but my memory says it wasn't. I'm confused and stressed about this inconsistency.
Wait, I just told my hallmate the link was fixed, but they actually said it's broken. That's messed up; now we're stuck again.
I'm so frustrated; I just dropped the essay call because my brain is fried, and now we're stuck with a broken link again. This is ridiculous.
I finally got the essay started after that link drama, but now I'm stressing about my sleep debt and running out of time.
I finally got the essay started, but now I'm stressing about my sleep debt and running out of time. This is ridiculous.

desires: Not have to explain why I'm unreliable to my hallmate or the professor again, especially since they keep getting info wrong.
Finish this scholarship application so I don't have to drag it out and burn out completely before Thursday.

goals.description: Finish the personal scholarship application and upload both files immediately so I can sleep without worrying about messing up hallmate_A or getting blamed for broken links again.
goals.rationale: I'm already stressed enough from this deadline; dragging it out until Thursday is a bad idea. I need to get this done now to protect my peace of mind and avoid having to explain why I'm unreliable. My brain is fried, so I'll just do the bare minimum viable stuff to close the loop on this.

goals.description: Go home, crash on the couch, and text hallmate_A a simple 'thanks' note if they ask, then completely drop the plan to join cohort_3 dinner since I don't have the energy to perform or bond over games right now.
goals.rationale: I let the fun window pass because trying to force a stream while exhausted feels wrong and will just lead to more regret. Survival mode wins tonight: I'll handle the social awkwardness and renegotiate the dinner plan later when I'm actually awake. Trying to bond over games right now is a waste of precious energy.

goals.description: Finish the scholarship application essay and upload documents immediately.
goals.rationale: I can't let my cohort know I'm broke. Even though this feels like a lot of work right now, getting the award approved means I don't have to ask them for money later. It's better to handle this alone now than to embarrass everyone next week.

goals.description: Skip the essay draft and go straight to the fun game stream with the hallmate tonight.
goals.rationale: I promised the hallmate I'd show up, and disappointing them is just too much pressure. I'll do that part tonight after I crush the application today. No point stressing about it if I've already got the deadline secured.

desires: Get the scholarship approved so I don't have to beg my cohort for money later
